interpretation.meaning_type: Quiet internal processing with depth

variants.text: Feelings often stack quietly before you name them. Time alone lets your nervous system assemble what happened so the meaning arrives intact instead of rushed.
Because you metabolize emotion on the inside first, the calm exterior is usually a processing stage. Once language arrives it tends to be precise, grounded, and hard to dismiss.

context.meaning_type: Quiet internal processing with depth

variants.text: Signal your tempo so others know the pause is intentional: say "Give me tonight to sit with this and I'll share what I find." Trust stays high while you honor inner sorting.
Build a translation ritual - a voice memo, a slow walk, a note in your journal - before you respond. It prevents the pause from being misread as distance and gives you a fuller story to bring back.

meta.meaning_type: Quiet internal processing with depth

variants.text: Signal your tempo so others know the pause is intentional: say "Give me tonight to sit with this and I'll share what I find." Trust stays high while you honor inner sorting.
Build a translation ritual - a voice memo, a slow walk, a note in your journal - before you respond. It prevents the pause from being misread as distance and gives you a fuller story to bring back.

interpretation.meaning_type: Fast activation and release

variants.text: Your system fires fast because feeling (A2) meets the need to release (A7). Emotions flare honestly in the moment yet complete quickly once they are expressed.
Emotional data travels in real time for you. The instant something matters, your body rings the bell; once acknowledged, it usually lets go without residue.

context.meaning_type: Fast activation and release

variants.text: Create micro rituals that let the surge move - step outside after a heated meeting, jot a one line journal entry, shake out your hands - so intensity does not leak into unrelated moments.
Tell trusted people that a strong reaction often means "this touched me" rather than "this still controls me." It keeps them from reading the peak as permanence and gives you room to reset.

meta.meaning_type: Fast activation and release

variants.text: Create micro rituals that let the surge move - step outside after a heated meeting, jot a one line journal entry, shake out your hands - so intensity does not leak into unrelated moments.
Tell trusted people that a strong reaction often means "this touched me" rather than "this still controls me." It keeps them from reading the peak as permanence and gives you room to reset.

observation.meaning_type: Steady emotions, slow to register change

interpretation.meaning_type: Steady emotions, slow to register change

variants.text: Depth meets pacing here: you stay steady because feelings need to cross an internal threshold before they register. Once they do, the insight is usually durable.
You often trust delayed clarity - body cues, patience, hindsight - because your system prefers confirmation over impulse. It keeps you calm yet can hide subtle discomfort until later.

context.meaning_type: Steady emotions, slow to register change

variants.text: Schedule a follow up space after meaningful interactions - walk home, journal, a body scan - so the slower wave lands before it turns into fatigue or withdrawal.
Let collaborators know you may send the "second read" later. It normalizes your pacing and ensures the accurate, slower insight still shapes the outcome.

meta.meaning_type: Steady emotions, slow to register change

variants.text: Schedule a follow up space after meaningful interactions - walk home, journal, a body scan - so the slower wave lands before it turns into fatigue or withdrawal.
Let collaborators know you may send the "second read" later. It normalizes your pacing and ensures the accurate, slower insight still shapes the outcome.

interpretation.meaning_type: High sensitivity to emotional atmosphere

variants.text: Your relational radar (A6) pairs with emotional depth (A2), so you register tone, posture, and distance before words explain anything. You often feel the weather in a room first.
The system scans for harmony and therefore absorbs extra material that is not yours. That sensitivity is not fragility - it is data gathering - but it needs outlets.

context.meaning_type: High sensitivity to emotional atmosphere

variants.text: Use decompression rituals after dense spaces - wash your hands, change rooms, sit in silence for five minutes - to return what is not yours and keep your own signal clear.
Name micro boundaries like "Let me reset for five minutes before we debrief." When your nervous system is not overloaded, the same sensitivity becomes a strategic advantage.

meta.meaning_type: High sensitivity to emotional atmosphere

variants.text: Use decompression rituals after dense spaces - wash your hands, change rooms, sit in silence for five minutes - to return what is not yours and keep your own signal clear.
Name micro boundaries like "Let me reset for five minutes before we debrief." When your nervous system is not overloaded, the same sensitivity becomes a strategic advantage.

interpretation.meaning_type: Emotion-first, meaning later

variants.text: Emotional data reaches you faster than mental conclusions. Feelings flag the truth before logic (A3) builds the case, which is why explanations may lag behind intuition.
Your body often delivers the yes or no before words are ready. Meaning is retroactive: once you trace the feeling, the rationale shows up and usually validates it.

context.meaning_type: Emotion-first, meaning later

variants.text: Capture the first feeling quickly - a word, color, or sensation - and circle back later to translate it. This keeps intuition intact while giving your mind a path to articulate it.
During decisions say, "My gut says this; give me time to map why." Partners get orientation, and you keep the early emotional guidance that protects you from overthinking.

meta.meaning_type: Emotion-first, meaning later

variants.text: Capture the first feeling quickly - a word, color, or sensation - and circle back later to translate it. This keeps intuition intact while giving your mind a path to articulate it.
During decisions say, "My gut says this; give me time to map why." Partners get orientation, and you keep the early emotional guidance that protects you from overthinking.

interpretation.meaning_type: Protected vulnerability

variants.text: Tender emotion (A2) meets A1's need for self possession, so vulnerability is selective. You open on your terms and only when the container feels respectful and well paced.
Your emotional life is deep, yet you preview spaces carefully. Trust is earned through reliability rather than intensity, and that caution protects the honesty you value.

context.meaning_type: Protected vulnerability

variants.text: Design small trust rituals - share a headline feeling in low stakes contexts, name the kind of response you need - so others learn how to meet you without pushing past your guard.
Use slower entry ramps such as written updates or one to one check ins before group exposure. You stay emotionally honest while keeping control over how much access others get.

meta.meaning_type: Protected vulnerability

variants.text: Design small trust rituals - share a headline feeling in low stakes contexts, name the kind of response you need - so others learn how to meet you without pushing past your guard.
Use slower entry ramps such as written updates or one to one check ins before group exposure. You stay emotionally honest while keeping control over how much access others get.
